steps: Navigate to https://lookerstudio.google.com/navigation/reporting and create a new blank report
'Add data to report' > BigQuery > project '{project_id}' > dataset '{dataset}' > table '{table}' > Add > confirm
Enable Responsive Layout

Take a screenshot (no filename) to get visual context. Click the 'Add chart in placeholder' button (class placeholder-add-chart-button) in the center of the empty section, then select '{chart_type}' from the chart picker.

Take a screenshot (no filename) to get visual context. Use evaluate() with this JS to click the section's add-chart button (avoids toolbar ambiguity): (function(){{var s=document.querySelectorAll('.section-container');var last=s[s.length-1];var btn=last.querySelector('.add-chart-button');if(btn){{btn.click();return 'clicked'}}return 'not found'}})()  Then select '{chart_type}' from the chart picker.

Take a screenshot (no filename) to get visual context. Click the 'Add control in placeholder' button (class placeholder-add-control-button) in the center of the empty section, then select '{chart_type}'.

Take a screenshot (no filename) to get visual context. Click on the current section to select it. Then click the section's own 'add a control' button (class add-control-button, NOT the toolbar). Select '{chart_type}'.

Take a screenshot (no filename) to get visual context. Use evaluate() with this JS to click the last add-section button: (function(){{var btns=document.querySelectorAll('.add-section-button');var last=btns[btns.length-1];if(last){{last.click();return 'clicked'}}return 'not found'}})()  Wait for the new empty section to appear.

Take a screenshot (no filename) to get visual context. Click the 'Add chart in placeholder' button in the center of the empty section, then select 'Text' from the chart picker. Set the text content to '{text_content}'.
Click 'open style menu', and set the 'arrangement' to 'stretch'
In the property panel, click the 'Setup' tab. Click the dimension chip (NOT the Data panel search bar), and select '{dimension_name}' from the field picker dropdown.
In the property panel, click the 'Setup' tab. Click 'Add dimension' and select '{dimension_name}' from the field picker dropdown.
In the property panel, click the 'Setup' tab. Click the metric chip (NOT the Data panel search bar), and select '{metric_name}' from the field picker dropdown.
In the property panel 'Setup' tab, click the metric chip (NOT the Data panel search bar), select '{metric_name}', then change it to {aggregation_type} of {metric_name}.
In the property panel, click the 'Setup' tab. Click 'Add metric' and select '{metric_name}' from the field picker dropdown.
In the property panel 'Setup' tab, click 'Add metric', select '{metric_name}', then change it to {aggregation_type} of {metric_name}.
In the Style tab, find 'Chart title' section, toggle 'Show title' on (mat-mdc-slide-toggle), then type '{title_text}' in the title text field.
In the Style tab, find the color picker (class color-preview-box) for Series 1 and set it to hex value '{color_value}'.
In the Style tab, find the font size dropdown and set it to '{font_size}'.
In the Style tab, find 'X-axis' section and toggle the axis title switch on (mat-mdc-slide-toggle).
In the Style tab, find 'Y-axis' section and toggle the axis title switch on (mat-mdc-slide-toggle).
In the Style tab, find 'Background and border' section and click 'Add border shadow' (aria-label 'Add border shadow').
In the Style tab, find 'Legend' section and set position to '{position}' using the dropdown.
In the Style tab, find 'Data labels' or 'Show data labels' and toggle it on (mat-mdc-slide-toggle).
In the Style tab, find 'Compact numbers' and toggle it on (mat-mdc-slide-toggle).
In the Style tab, find 'Background and border' section, click the Background color picker (class color-preview-box), and set it to hex value '{color_value}'.
In the property panel 'Setup' tab, scroll down to the 'Sort' section, set the sort field to '{sort_field}' and order to '{sort_order}'.
In the property panel 'Setup' tab, scroll down and set the row limit (aria-label 'Rows per page') to {row_limit}.
In the property panel 'Style' tab, {others}

In the property panel 'Setup' tab, scroll down to find the filter section. Click 'Add a filter' to open the filter dialog. Set type to {filter_type}, set field to '{field_name}', set condition to '{condition}', set value to '{value}', then click Save.

In the property panel 'Setup' tab, scroll down to find the filter section. Click 'Add a filter' to open the filter dialog. Set type to {filter_type}, set field to '{field_name}', select condition 'Between (>= && <=)' from the dropdown, enter '{value_start}' in the first value field and '{value_end}' in the second value field, then click Save.
In the property panel 'Setup' tab, click the metric '{metric_name}' chip to open its options, then change the aggregation to '{aggregation_type}'.
In the property panel 'Setup' tab, scroll down to 'Chart interactions' and toggle 'Cross-filtering' on.

In the Data panel (far right), click 'Add a field' (aria-label 'Add a field', class add-field-button), then click 'Add a calculated field'. Type '{field_name}' in the Field Name input, type '{formula}' in the Formula editor, click Save, wait 2 seconds, then click Done.
In the Style tab, find the font color picker and set it to hex value '{color_value}'.
Click on the 'Add report title' placeholder text field on the canvas, clear it, and type '{report_title}'.
Click on the report title text 'Untitled Report' at the top-left of the editor, clear it, and type '{report_title}'.
